palette_hint: Background #F2EDE6 (warm off-white newsprint), primary text #1A1208 (near-black), accent #C0392B (alert red) and #2E5E8E (geologic blue) for data emphasis and section headers.
type_style: Headings in Georgia serif, bold and condensed-feeling at large sizes; body in system-ui at 400 weight, 1.6 line-height; data callouts in monospace (Courier New) for numerical authority; overall feel is rigorous investigative broadsheet.
description: A special-report digital atlas investigating the Pacific Rim's underreported seismic corridors, combining field data, survivor testimony, and policy analysis into a five-chapter deep-dive titled 'Deep Fault: The Earthquakes Nobody Is Preparing For'.
constraints: Each page must have at least 3 visually distinct layout regions: a full-bleed header band (#1A1208 background, white type), a multi-column editorial body (max-width 1100px, centered, CSS grid with asymmetric columns), and a data-sidebar or callout panel using a separate flex layout — no two regions share the same grid system.

A consistent spacing scale must be applied: base unit 8px, with section gaps at 48px, internal component padding at 24px, and tight data-label padding at 8px 12px — these values appear literally in CSS.

Accent color #C0392B is reserved exclusively for statistical pull-quotes, warning labels, and section-break rules (3px horizontal rules); #2E5E8E is used only for map-region labels, chart axis text, and hyperlink-style inline data references.

Typography rhythm breaks are mandatory between sections: each section transition uses a full-width 1px rule in #BFB8AE followed by 48px whitespace before the next heading, creating a deliberate visual pause.

No decorative gradients or box-shadows; visual hierarchy is achieved purely through type scale (ranging from 11px data labels to 56px hero numerals), weight contrast, color discipline, and whitespace.

page_specs.overview: The landing chapter opens with a full-bleed black header containing the report title 'Deep Fault' in 56px Georgia bold and a 14px monospace dateline ('Published: March 2025 — Updated Quarterly'). Below, a three-column CSS grid presents: a 660-word editorial lede on the Cascadia, Hikurangi, and Manila Trench subduction zones; a right-rail sidebar with five key statistics (each number in 36px monospace red) covering estimated populations at risk, years since last major rupture, and preparedness-index scores; and a bottom-spanning 'Chapter Navigator' flex row linking all five sections with short 20-word teasers per chapter.
page_specs.fault-maps: This page renders four distinct ASCII-art-style schematic 'maps' built entirely from CSS grid cells and background-color fills (no img tags) showing the Cascadia, Hikurangi, Manila, and Makran fault corridors, each labeled in #2E5E8E monospace. A two-column editorial grid beside each map provides 150-word geologic summaries noting fault length in kilometers, average slip rate in mm/year, and last major rupture date. A bottom panel uses a horizontal flex layout to display a 'Magnitude Comparison' bar chart built from CSS width-percentage div bars in #2E5E8E, with Courier New axis labels and a red annotation marking the 9.2 threshold.
page_specs.risk-zones: Three asymmetric grid regions structure this chapter: a wide left column (70%) with a 900-word investigative narrative on why building codes in Padang, Seaside (Oregon), and Batangas remain inadequate; a narrow right column (30%) housing a stacked flex list of 12 'Risk Tier' city cards, each card a bordered box with city name in bold Georgia, tier rating in large red monospace, and a two-line policy note in 13px system-ui. A full-width bottom section breaks into a four-cell grid displaying 'Preparedness Score' gauges built from nested CSS border and background techniques, one per city cluster, with scores expressed as fractions (e.g. '3 / 10') in 48px monospace.
page_specs.human-cost: The emotional core of the report, this page leads with a pull-quote band: full-width #1A1208 background, a survivor testimony from the 2009 Padang earthquake in 28px italic Georgia white, attributed in 12px monospace. Below, a three-column grid presents six survivor profile blocks, each containing a 60-word testimony excerpt, a bold-name header, and a location tag in #2E5E8E; the grid uses dense 16px gaps for a documentary-contact-sheet feel. A separate full-width section at the bottom uses a timeline built in CSS flex (horizontal scroll disabled, wrapping allowed) showing eight seismic events from 1960 to 2024, with event name, magnitude, and death toll in alternating #C0392B and #2E5E8E type on an #F2EDE6 background.
page_specs.what-next: The policy and action chapter opens with a two-column split: left column holds a 700-word 'Five Reforms' editorial with numbered headings in 22px Georgia bold and body paragraphs in system-ui; right column presents a 'Policy Scorecard' table built with CSS grid (5 columns: Country, Early Warning, Building Code, Public Drills, Funding) where each cell is color-coded — #C0392B for 'Failing', #2E5E8E for 'Partial', #1A1208 text on #BFB8AE for 'Adequate'. A closing full-bleed band in #2E5E8E contains the report credits, methodology note in 13px system-ui white, and a monospace citation block listing six academic sources with journal names, volume numbers, and page ranges.
